sentence: Las casas están tapadas por la lluvia.
ipa: lah ˈkasah eˈhtaŋ taˈpaðah poɾ la ˈʝuβja
gloss_en: The houses are shut because of the rain.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi pana comió arroz con pescado en la playa.
ipa: mi ˈpana koˈmio aˈros koŋ peˈhkaðo eŋ la plaˈʝa
gloss_en: My buddy ate rice with fish on the beach.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El niño pequeño juega en la plaza los domingos.
ipa: el ˈniɲo peˈkeɲo ˈxweɣa eŋ la ˈplasa loh doˈminɡoh
gloss_en: The little boy plays in the square on Sundays.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Vamos a comer una arepa con queso amarillo?
ipa: ˈbamoh a koˈmeɾ una aˈɾepa koŋ ˈkeso amaˈɾiʝo
gloss_en: Shall we eat an arepa with yellow cheese?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Ese chamo es muy chévere y trabajador.
ipa: ˈese ˈtʃamo ˈeh ˈmui ˈtʃeβeɾe i tɾaβaxaˈðoɾ
gloss_en: That kid is really cool and hard-working.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening) per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: La luz del faro brilla sobre el mar en calma.
ipa: la ˈlus del ˈfaɾo ˈbɾiʝa ˈsobɾe el ˈmaɾ eŋ ˈkalma
gloss_en: The lighthouse beam shines over the calm sea.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per penny2002.

sentence: Compré diez cambures maduros en el mercado.
ipa: koˈmpɾe ˈdjes kaˈmbuɾeh maˈðuɾoh eŋ el meˈɾkaðo
gloss_en: I bought ten ripe bananas at the market.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: El corazón del pueblo late con música y baile.
ipa: el koɾaˈsoŋ del ˈpweblo ˈlate koŋ ˈmusika i ˈbajle
gloss_en: The heart of the town beats with music and dance.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: Los pescadores salen al alba con sus redes.
ipa: loh pehkaˈðoɾeh ˈsaleŋ al ˈalba koŋ suh ˈreðeh
gloss_en: The fishermen set out at dawn with their nets.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Cuántas personas vienen a la fiesta esta noche?
ipa: ˈkuantah peˈɾsonah ˈbjeneŋ a la ˈfjehta ˈehta ˈnotʃe
gloss_en: How many people are coming to the party tonight?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: La región montañosa guarda muchos pueblos antiguos.
ipa: la reˈxioŋ montaˈɲosa ˈɡwaɾda ˈmutʃoh ˈpwebloh aˈntiɣwoh
gloss_en: The mountainous region holds many old villages.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El perro corre detrás del carro por la avenida.
ipa: el ˈpero ˈkore deˈtɾah del ˈkaro poɾ la aβeˈniða
gloss_en: The dog runs after the car along the avenue.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); alveolar trill [r] preserved per penny2002.

sentence: No quiero que llueva sobre las montañas mañana.
ipa: ˈno ˈkjeɾo ke ˈʝweβa ˈsobɾe lah moˈntaɲah maˈɲana
gloss_en: I don't want it to rain over the mountains tomorrow.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi hermano vive lejos, junto al río grande.
ipa: mi eˈɾmano ˈbiβe ˈlexoh ˈxunto al ˈrio ˈɡɾande
gloss_en: My brother lives far away, next to the big river.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); alveolar trill [r] preserved per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: La mujer canta una tonada vieja y hermosa.
ipa: la muˈxeɾ ˈkanta una toˈnaða ˈbjexa i eˈɾmosa
gloss_en: The woman sings an old and beautiful tune.
notes: Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per penny2002.

sentence: Dame un vaso de agua fría, por favor.
ipa: ˈdame uŋ ˈbaso de ˈaɣwa ˈfɾia poɾ faˈβoɾ
gloss_en: Give me a glass of cold water, please.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Aquellos árboles dan sombra en el patio.
ipa: aˈkeʝoh ˈaɾboleh ˈdaŋ ˈsombɾa eŋ el ˈpatjo
gloss_en: Those trees give shade in the courtyard.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El señor mayor guarda historias de su juventud.
ipa: el seˈɲoɾ maˈʝoɾ ˈɡwaɾda iˈhtoɾjah de su xuβeˈntu
gloss_en: The old gentleman keeps stories of his youth.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per penny2002.

sentence: Las estrellas alumbran el llano durante la noche.
ipa: lah eˈhtɾeʝah aˈlumbɾaŋ el ˈʝano duˈɾante la ˈnotʃe
gloss_en: The stars light up the plain during the night.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Buenos días, ¿cómo durmió usted hoy?
ipa: ˈbwenoh ˈdiah ˈkomo duˈɾmio uˈhte ˈoi
gloss_en: Good morning, how did you sleep today?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.
